title: silene vulgaris var. oleracea
intro: Silene vulgaris var. oleracea, also known as catchfly or bladder campion, is a perennial herb in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) that grows in damp grasslands and meadows. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Silene vulgaris var. oleracea include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or by making a tincture with the root and stems, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
